paragraph_content.content: Solution: (a) and (b) are spherically complete. In fact, every finite extension of

is spherically complete. Such a field is discretely valued and complete. In this case, a decreasing sequence of closed balls either eventually stabilizes, or has radius converging to 0. In both cases, the intersection is nonempty.

item_content.content: (c) is not spherically complete. Notice that spherical completeness implies completeness. (Why? From any Cauchy sequence, one can construct a decreasing sequence of closed balls whose intersection gives the limit of the Cauchy sequence.) However, it is well-known that
is not complete, hence not spherically complete.
